name: Backbone.js
description: A lightweight JavaScript library providing structure with Models, Views, Collections, and Events for building client-side applications

description.value: A lightweight JavaScript library providing structure with Models, Views, Collections, and Events for building client-side applications

initial-release.comment: First released October 13, 2010. Created by Jeremy Ashkenas, also known for CoffeeScript and Underscore.js.

latest-release.comment: Version 1.6.0 released February 5, 2024; version 1.6.1 followed shortly after

active-maintenance.comment: Last release was in early 2024. The project is in maintenance mode - functional but not actively developed.

reactivity-model.comment: Backbone uses an event-based system rather than a reactive model. Models emit events on changes that Views subscribe to manually.

component-model.comment: Views are class-based, extending Backbone.View. No component abstraction - Views manage DOM regions directly.

rendering-paradigm.comment: Backbone is view-agnostic and imperative. You manually update the DOM in response to model events. No templating is required by the framework.

state-management-approach.comment: Models and Collections provide built-in state management with events. External libraries not required.

opinionatedness.comment: Backbone is intentionally minimal and unopinionated. It provides building blocks but leaves architecture decisions to developers.

backward-compatibility.comment: Very stable API since 1.0 (2013). Apps written years ago still work on current versions.

typescript-support.comment: Type definitions available via @types/backbone. Not native TypeScript but usable with external types.

template-syntax.comment: View-agnostic - commonly used with Underscore.js templates, Handlebars, or Mustache. No built-in template syntax.

styling-approach.comment: No built-in styling solution. Uses standard CSS.

build-required.comment: Can be used directly via CDN without a build step. Just include the script tag.

reactive-primitives.comment: No reactive primitives. Uses event-based system where you manually listen to model/collection events.

computed-values.comment: No built-in computed values. Derived state must be calculated manually.

two-way-binding.comment: No built-in two-way binding. Manual event handling required to sync views with models.

event-handling.comment: Declarative event hash in Views: events: {'click .button': 'onClick'}

conditional-rendering.comment: No built-in conditional rendering. Handled in template engines or manual DOM manipulation.

list-rendering.comment: Collections exist but list rendering is handled manually or via template engines.

component-slots.comment: No component/slot abstraction. Views are standalone DOM managers.

teleport-portal.comment: No portal/teleport concept. Views render to their el element.

error-boundaries.comment: No error boundary concept. Standard try-catch error handling.

context-provide-inject.comment: No dependency injection system. Shared state typically done via globals or manual passing.

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: this.el and this.$el provide direct DOM access. this.$() for scoped jQuery queries.

official-router.comment: Backbone.Router is included in the core library

nested-routes.comment: Basic router without nested route support. Backbone.RouteManager extension adds nested routes.

dynamic-routes.comment: Supports dynamic segments with :param syntax and optional parameters in parentheses

route-guards.comment: No built-in before/after guards. RouteManager extension adds this functionality.

lazy-loading-routes.comment: No built-in lazy loading. Must be implemented manually with dynamic imports.

scroll-restoration.comment: No built-in scroll restoration. Browser default or manual implementation.

built-in-state.comment: Backbone.Model provides reactive local state with get/set and event emission

built-in-global-state.comment: No official global state solution. Typically done via shared models or Marionette's application patterns.

global-state-library.comment: No official global state library. Marionette.js is commonly used for larger application architecture.

devtools-state-inspection.comment: Backbone Debugger Chrome extension shows models, collections, views, and routers in real-time

state-persistence.comment: No built-in persistence. Backbone.sync handles REST API sync but not local storage.

official-cli.comment: No official CLI. Use general JavaScript tooling or Yeoman generators.

hot-module-replacement.comment: No built-in HMR. Depends on bundler configuration.

browser-devtools.comment: Backbone Debugger Chrome extension available, though community-maintained

vs-code-extension.comment: No dedicated VS Code extension. Standard JavaScript tooling applies.

error-messages.comment: Basic error messages. Errors typically come from JavaScript runtime, not framework-specific guidance.

documentation-quality.comment: Annotated source code serves as documentation. Clear but dated API docs. No modern tutorials.

interactive-tutorial.comment: No official interactive tutorial. Various third-party tutorials exist but are dated.

playground.comment: No official playground. Can use JSFiddle or CodePen.

bundle-size-core.comment: ~8 kB minified + gzipped for backbone.js core (requires Underscore.js dependency)

bundle-size-full.comment: ~24 kB including Underscore.js and jQuery dependencies; actual size depends on jQuery version

virtual-dom.comment: No virtual DOM. Direct DOM manipulation via jQuery or native APIs.

compile-time-optimization.comment: No compilation step. Runtime library only.

tree-shaking.comment: Single bundle, not tree-shakeable. Designed as a complete library.

code-splitting.comment: No built-in code splitting. Depends on bundler.

lazy-components.comment: No built-in lazy loading for views.

ssr-capable.comment: Designed for client-side only. No SSR support.

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Not included in modern js-framework-benchmark due to its age and different paradigm

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: ~745,000 weekly downloads. Still widely used in legacy codebases.

component-libraries.comment: No dedicated UI component libraries. Backbone is view-agnostic and uses any DOM/jQuery plugins.

meta-framework.comment: Marionette.js provides structure for larger Backbone applications with regions, layouts, and better view management

major-companies-using.comment: Many companies used Backbone historically; some still maintain legacy codebases

official-sponsor-backer.comment: Originally developed at DocumentCloud. Community-maintained since.

discord-community-size.comment: No active Discord community. Discussions happen on Stack Overflow and GitHub.

stack-overflow-questions.comment: Approximately 24,000 questions tagged with backbone.js, reflecting historical popularity

job-market-demand.comment: Niche demand, primarily for maintaining legacy applications. Few new projects use Backbone.

official-testing-library.comment: No official testing library. Works with any JavaScript testing framework.

testing-library-name.comment: Framework-agnostic. Commonly tested with Jasmine, Mocha, or Jest. Sinon.js often used for mocking.

component-testing.comment: Views can be unit tested by instantiating and checking DOM output

e2e-testing-integration.comment: Works with any E2E testing framework like Cypress or Playwright

snapshot-testing.comment: Supported via Jest or other snapshot testing tools

mobile-framework.comment: No official mobile framework. Can be used with Cordova/PhoneGap.

pwa-support.comment: No built-in PWA support. Standard PWA techniques apply.

desktop-framework.comment: No official desktop framework. Can be used with Electron.

learning-curve.comment: Relatively gentle learning curve due to minimal API. Just Models, Views, Collections, and Router to learn.

concepts-to-learn.comment: Models, Views, Collections, Router, Events - core concepts are few and well-documented

time-to-hello-world.comment: ~5 minutes. Can include via CDN and start immediately.

migration-path.comment: No official migration guides. Actually, other frameworks provide guides TO Backbone, not from it.

incremental-adoption.comment: Excellent for incremental adoption. Can add structure to any existing page without full rewrite.
